user.title: IDMate
user.description: What do you want to set up?

menu_options.charge: Charge tracker (15-min meter readings via HTTP)
menu_options.import_vehicles: Import IDMate vehicles into Home Assistant (HTTP)

telemetry.title: IDMate Telemetry — MQTT connection
telemetry.description: Connect to the IDMate MQTT broker. The AES key is the same 64-character hex key as on the IDMate server (MQTT_AES_KEY).

data.device: Device name (IDMate vehicle id, e.g. sirius)
data.interval: Evaluation interval / minimum gap between sends (s)

data_description.device: Must match the vehicle id in IDMate (publishes to tele/<device>/data).
data_description.host: The MQTT broker's LAN IP/host reachable from Home Assistant.
data_description.username: Leave empty if your broker allows anonymous connections.
data_description.password: Leave empty for an anonymous broker.
data_description.aes_key: The exact same 64-char key as the IDMate server's MQTT_AES_KEY.
data_description.tls_insecure: Keep on for a self-signed broker certificate.

entities.title: IDMate Telemetry — entity mapping
entities.description: Pick the source entities. SoC and speed are required; the rest are optional. Range/power/odometer units are auto-detected (m/km/mi, W/kW).

charge.title: IDMate Charge Tracker
charge.description: Every quarter hour the consumption of the energy meter is posted to IDMate, which builds charge sessions automatically. Meter is required; price/base-fee are optional €/kWh entities (e.g. a Tibber/EPEX template sensor).

data_description.meter_entity: A continuous (always-increasing) kWh meter — the wallbox/meter total.
data_description.vehicle_plate: Used when no vehicle entity is set, or when it is unavailable.

import_vehicles.title: Import IDMate vehicles
import_vehicles.description: Pulls the vehicles you exposed in IDMate (Admin → Home Assistant) into HA. First open that page, generate a token and tick the vehicles to share — then paste the values below.

data_description.import_url: Just type the server's LAN IP, e.g. 192.168.188.5 — http:// and :3004 are added automatically. Shown on the IDMate admin page.
data_description.import_token: Copy it from IDMate → Admin → Home Assistant (the 'Read token' field).
data_description.import_interval: How often to refresh, in seconds. 30 is fine.

error.aes_key_length: The AES key must be exactly 64 hex characters (32 bytes).
error.aes_key_hex: The AES key must contain only hex characters (0-9, a-f).
error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the IDMate server at this URL.
error.invalid_auth: The IDMate server rejected this token.
error.not_configured: IDMate has no Home Assistant export configured yet (set a token in IDMate admin).

init.title: IDMate — options
init.description: Adjust the mapping (and, for telemetry, the send interval).

data.interval: Evaluation interval / minimum gap between sends (s)
data.name: Name (unique, e.g. wallbox)
